opp.name: GoTyme CLM
opp.next_step: 10% confidence 16th Mar - intro mail sent and and requesting for revival meeting. 14th Jan- needs to be requalified- original enthusiasm has waned 9th Nov Connecting with Kris on 12th while in Manila- CLM requirement is urgent , with strong push from Sth Africa BU 24th Oct Spoke with CPO at Summit- very keen to move on CLM for local and 4 countries (including Sth Africa). Sense of urgency. Carl to arrange on line demo and contract discussions already underway Specific next steps would typically involve engaging with GoTyme Bank to progress in the evaluation of Zycus’ procurement tools or aligning with their upcoming initiatives such as the integration of new digital banking services.
opp.ais_why:
• Across 692 chars of NextStep covering four dated entries from Oct 2024 to Mar 2026 ‚Äî including CPO summit meeting, CLM urgency from South Africa BU, and a more recent enthusiasm cooling ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic or GenAI. Product scope is iContract only (no AI SKUs). Per the 14 Jan 2026 entry: 'needs to be requalified- original enthusiasm has waned.'
• On 24 Oct: 'Spoke with CPO at Summit- very keen to move on CLM for local and 4 countries (including Sth Africa). Sense of urgency. Carl to arrange on line demo and contract discussions already underway.' On 9 Nov: 'Connecting with Kris on 12th while in Manila- CLM requirement is urgent, with strong push from Sth Africa BU.' On 16 Mar 2026: 'intro mail sent and requesting for revival meeting.' Initially urgent CLM ask covering local + 4 countries, but the enthusiasm has cooled to requalification status.
• Confidence is Medium. GoTyme has iContract as the only product in scope ‚Äî focused CLM-only deal ‚Äî and the enthusiasm has reversed from 'sense of urgency' to needing requalification. Score 3 (AI Resistant) reflects the single-CLM product scope with no AI dimension.

items.quote: No Avoma calls found via opp, account, or attendee-email discovery.
CPO referenced at Summit (24 Oct 2025) but not named and holds no Contact Role.
Urgency stated but no cost, volume, cycle-time, or compliance figure documented.
Coupa named but no recent sentiment, pricing, or bake-off status.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists in Salesforce and none is recoverable from calls (zero buyer calls on record). The Next Step log states 'very keen to move on CLM for local and 4 countries (including South Africa)' (24 Oct 2025) and 'CLM requirement is urgent, with strong push from the relevant stakeholder' (09 Nov 2025), which frames scope and urgency but names no cost, contract volume, cycle-time, or compliance-risk figure. The owner's own '10% confidence' read confirms weak qualification. Quantifying the CLM pain (contract count, manual cycle time, audit or compliance exposure across the 4 entities) is the move that would justify a revival.

sources: SF Next_Step__c '(16 Mar 2026) intro mail sent and requesting for revival meeting'
champion.narrative: No champion is identified. Kris Galicinao (Head of Procure-to-Play) is the sole buyer-side Contact Role and is marked Decision Maker, but there is no evidence of advocacy, multi-threading on our behalf, or active sponsorship. The last activity was 11 Feb 2026 (142 days ago) and Kris did not reply to the 16 Mar 2026 revival email, while '10% confidence' and 'enthusiasm has waned' (14 Jan 2026) confirm weak internal support. Kris was engaged in late 2025 but has since gone silent, so the deal is single-threaded on a cooled contact. Restarting requires re-engaging Kris directly and, if unresponsive, opening a parallel thread to a procurement peer or the budget owner.

sources: No Avoma calls; competitive intel is stale
competition.narrative: Coupa is named in Competitors__c, but no recent competitive intelligence (pricing, sentiment, bake-off status, or the buyer's lean) is documented, and there are no calls to read it from. The 24 Oct 2025 note that the CPO was 'very keen to move on CLM' with 'contract discussions already underway' implies Zycus may have led or been sole-sourced at that point, but the 142-day silence since Feb 2026 makes it impossible to confirm whether Coupa re-engaged or the deal stalled for non-competitive reasons (budget, priority shift). There is no evidence the buyer prefers Coupa. On re-engagement the wedge is iContract's focused, faster-to-deploy CLM for a 4-country rollout versus Coupa's broader suite complexity.

sources: SF Next_Step__c '(14 Jan 2026) enthusiasm has waned'
identify_pain.narrative: The pain is directionally clear but not operationally detailed. The Next Step log shows an urgent CLM need spanning 'local and 4 countries (including South Africa)' (24 Oct 2025) with 'strong push from the relevant stakeholder' (09 Nov 2025), pointing to manual contract management across multiple entities and a regional expansion driver. However no specific operational or financial pain metric is quantified, and by 14 Jan 2026 the 'original enthusiasm has waned', suggesting the pain was not urgent enough to sustain momentum or a competing priority displaced it. The pain owner is partly known (Kris Galicinao in procurement, plus an unnamed South Africa BU stakeholder); re-validating whether the 4-country CLM pain is still live is the move.

sources: No Salesforce Tasks/Events in last 120 days
paper_process.narrative: No paper process is documented (no MSA, SOW, legal, procurement, or InfoSec activity in the 120-day window). GoTyme Bank is a regulated digital bank, so a data-privacy and InfoSec gate (Philippines and South Africa regulatory scope) plus banking-platform integration review are near-certain, yet none appear in the record. The 24 Oct 2025 'contract discussions already underway' note never converted to logged contracting activity. Visibility would come from re-opening the thread and asking the buyer to name their contracting and security-review path.

sources: SF Next_Step__c '(24 Oct 2025) Spoke with CPO at Summit'
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped on the opportunity. The only clue is the 24 Oct 2025 Next Step entry: 'Spoke with CPO at Summit - very keen to move on CLM ... contract discussions already underway', which shows a CPO with budget appetite existed at that time, but that person is not named, holds no Contact Role, and has not re-engaged. The single Contact Role, Kris Galicinao (Head of Procure-to-Play), is a Decision Maker, not the budget owner. Naming and reaching the CLM budget owner (the CPO or a finance sponsor) is a prerequisite before any commercial step.

sources: SF Next_Step__c '(24 Oct 2025) contract discussions already underway'
SF Field history: CloseDate pushed 14 Jan and 16 Mar 2026
decision_process.narrative: The buyer's decision process is not visible. The 24 Oct 2025 Next Step said 'contract discussions already underway', suggesting an early paper track, but nothing followed and the deal went dark after 11 Feb 2026. No approval chain, evaluation timeline, or committee is documented, and there are no calls to read the process from. For a regulated digital bank a multi-approver process is likely; confirming who signs and on what timeline is a re-qualification gap, not a picklist entry.

sources: No Avoma calls; no Business_Requirements evidence on record
decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria are documented and none are recoverable from calls. The deal sits in Formal Evaluation, which implies a criteria set exists on the buyer side, but no requirements list, RFP response, or use-case scope is captured in Salesforce and no buyer call names one. With enthusiasm logged as waned (14 Jan 2026), the criteria may never have been formalized. Re-establishing what GoTyme will actually judge CLM vendors on (multi-country contract support, local-language/regulatory fit, integration to their banking stack) is required to re-qualify.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: GoTyme Bank — Formal Evaluation · $120K · closes 31 Jan

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 22 is carried by competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15). Offsetting it: preference strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum is flat at 50: no forward or backward motion was observed.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 1 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 5 is anchored on the stage win (22), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 3 / AIS_Status Negative. Product scope is iContract only with no AI dimension, and the Next Step history over Oct 2024 to Mar 2026 never mentions AI, Merlin, or GenAI. No calls to test whether the field under- or over-rates appetite; the field read stands.

business_case.evidence: Urgency and 4-country scope stated (Oct-Nov 2025) but no ROI, savings, or quantified pain articulated by the buyer.

Close date pushed 30 Jan 2026 to 19 Aug 2026; owner reassigned to Laurice Quintero
Enthusiasm logged as waned; deal flagged for requalification at 10% confidence
Last buyer activity recorded; deal has been silent since
Revival email sent and close date pushed to 31 Jan 2027; no buyer reply

deal_movement.summary: The deal peaked in Oct-Nov 2025 (urgency, contract discussions underway, amount raised to $120k, stage to Formal Evaluation) then reversed: enthusiasm waned by 14 Jan 2026, close date was pushed twice, ownership churned, and the account has been silent since 11 Feb 2026 despite a 16 Mar revival email.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 01 Jul 2025 (367 days old). Last buyer activity 11 Feb 2026 (142 days ago). Close date 31 Jan 2027 (212 days out) and was twice pushed (30 Jan to 19 Aug 2026 on 14 Jan, then to 31 Jan 2027 on 16 Mar). Stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation on 24 Oct 2025, so ~8 months in Formal Evaluation with no advance. Cold for 60+ days forces Off Track regardless of stage or a distant close date.
evidence: LastActivityDate 2026-02-11 (142 days ago); no buyer-facing deliverable since
Next Step log: '10% confidence', '14th Jan - needs to be requalified - original enthusiasm has waned', '16th Mar - intro mail sent and requesting for revival meeting' (no buyer reply)
Close date pushed twice (14 Jan and 16 Mar 2026); Stage Formal Evaluation with Pipeline forecast
No Avoma calls discovered via opp, account, or attendee-email pulls
No Salesforce Tasks or Events in the last 120 days
Owner reassigned twice in 2026 (to Laurice Quintero 05 Jan, back to Carl Kimball 28 Apr), consistent with a dropped thread
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal dark 142 days with no buyer reply since 11 Feb 2026. The rep's 16 Mar revival email went unanswered, enthusiasm was logged as waning by 14 Jan 2026, and the sole contact has cooled with no forward motion in Formal Evaluation.

flags: Single-thread risk: the entire deal rests on one cooled Decision Maker (Kris Galicinao); open a second procurement thread now.
Economic buyer unmapped: name and reach the CLM budget owner (the CPO or finance sponsor) before any pricing step.
No business case: quantify the 4-country CLM pain (contract volume, cycle time, compliance risk) to justify a revival.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a dark, single-threaded early-stage deal that needs requalification before any commercial motion. The win levers are rebuilding a live thread, mapping the budget owner, and re-anchoring the CLM business case.

customer_preference.evidence: No recent buyer evidence; enthusiasm logged as waned (14 Jan 2026) and no reply since 11 Feb 2026.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the only named rival (Competitors__c) and there is no evidence the buyer leans to it; the competitive picture is dormant, not adverse. In Oct 2025 Zycus appeared to lead or be sole-sourced ('contract discussions already underway'), but 142 days of silence make current standing unknown, so the primary risk is the deal going cold, not a competitor winning. On re-engagement the wedge is iContract's focused CLM for a 4-country rollout versus Coupa's broader-suite complexity and cost.

competitors.how_we_win: Position iContract's faster time-to-value for focused CLM across the 4-country rollout versus Coupa's full-suite complexity and enterprise-tier cost; emphasize lower implementation risk for an emerging-market bank.
